trajectory.description: First iteration: create model.py (GINE+VirtualNode), train.py (with NaN masking, pos_weight for imbalance), predict.py. Train 5 epochs for quick baseline and time measurement.

Observation: exit code 139 (SIGSEGV/OOM) with empty stdout after 502s. Analysis: The compute_pos_weight function looped through 350K graphs × 128 tasks in Python (~45M iterations), likely causing extremely slow execution and eventual OOM/timeout. Also, pre-creating 350K Data objects wastes memory. Decision: Vectorize pos_weight using torch.stack, use lazy MolDataset class, add flush prints and PYTHONUNBUFFERED for debugging, reduce num_workers to 2 for stability.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training started successfully (data loaded, pos_weight computed, model created with 3.3M params), but evaluation crashed with 'ValueError: Input contains NaN' - model predictions contain NaN values. Analysis: The GINEConvManual or global_mean_pool_manual likely produces NaN for edge cases (empty graphs, single-node graphs, or numerical overflow in BatchNorm). Also, the model output might have Inf values that become NaN after sigmoid. Decision: Fix by (1) clamping model outputs to prevent Inf/NaN, (2) replacing NaN predictions with 0.5 in evaluation, (3) adding robustness to scatter operations, (4) ensure complete train.py is written (it was truncated).

trajectory.description: Observation: Val AP=0.1996 after 30 epochs, still improving steadily. Loss still declining. Model is undertrained and could benefit from larger capacity. Analysis: (1) hidden_dim=300 is small, increase to 512. (2) 30 epochs not enough, need 60+. (3) Cosine decay too aggressive, need warmup. (4) Add JumpingKnowledge for better multi-layer feature use. (5) Lower pos_weight cap (50 vs 100) for stability. Decision: Train larger model with better hyperparameters, submit eval for current best to get test baseline.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best test AP=0.2136 with linear feature encoding. Val AP plateaued around 0.22. The model treats categorical atom/bond features (atomic number, chirality, etc.) as continuous integers via linear layers, which is fundamentally wrong. OGB SOTA models use categorical embeddings. Analysis: The biggest improvement opportunity is replacing linear feature encoding with proper categorical embeddings. This should significantly improve representation learning. Decision: Rewrite model.py with categorical embeddings (using offset-based efficient embedding lookup), train from scratch for 80 epochs, submit eval.

Decision: Fix model.py clamp calls, restart training for 80 epochs.

Analysis: Need to rewrite model.py with correct forward pass (using `vn_feat[batch]` for proper shape [N, H]), resume training from best_v2.pt with EMA for 25 epochs. Time remaining ~41 min, ~68s/epoch = ~36 epochs possible. Decision: Fix model.py forward pass, train 25 epochs with EMA and cosine LR, submit eval.
